reviews.review_msg: Paticipation counts but gives no space for contribution. get ready to read. a lot. really heavy course- heaviest I've had at SFU yet

he's so nice and tbh even if you don't go to class its fine but there are pop quizzes so you should probably go to class just incase. if you do the readings beforehand you won't have any problems understanding the lectures, assignments weren't hard or graded hard either.

Had him for Summer 2024, and he was a great professor! The class was very interesting and what made it better was his vast knowledge on history as well as his sense of humour, I personally loved it. It was a chill semester with couple group assignments but very short, and there are pop quizzes but those are not too hard if you've done the readings!

one of the most helpful profs at sfu. Will always any questions you have and be willing to help you out without hesitation. This class (Berlin) was challenging for me but prof Niall made it easy and enjoyable. Quizzes are a bit difficult but there are many other chances to redeem yourself.

-The assignments were ok, but the lectures were incredibly dry and useless. ( everything was based on readings/films) -He promised to make a ppt for midterm review, but did not make it. ( also he assigned the midterm one week before finals) -The course felt disorganized & he was very unapproachable. Im super happy that its over now.
Great guy and I would take his class again.  Very reading heavy but is a very fair marker and accessible outside of class hours.
Wayne was awesome. Best feedback I have received and makes a material that can be long feel short. Enjoyed both papers and almost every class.
Lots of reading, but Wayne is the nicest prof I have had at SFU. He legit cares about what he's teaching and you can tell.

horrible communication from this prof. no matter how hard you work in her class she will always tell you it's "not good enough". completley unreasonable grading systems. if u ask what to study ahead of the exam she will provide the wrong info. not on top of her own job but expects u to be for her. doesnt reply to emails when she messes up.

lectures are irrelevant + insane amount of reading that doesn't even have any relevance. says to study 1 thing then makes the test the opposite. doesnt stick to own grading scheme. makes u write a long 10 page essay for just 5% of grade. makes 4 huge unrelated assignments due same day as final exam. very inconsiderate + disorganized. lost my exam

I took hum340 humanities and philosophy and dropped it after 2 clasaes cus it was too hard for me... But he seems really really friendly and he is open to different opinions and really listens and tries to understand each students opinions

Fun lectures, but lacks clarity for assignments-- this evidently dropped my grade because he didn't explain what he is looking for even when I asked. He also never explained about his "quiz" portion of the class, nor did he say when they'll be up. Syllabus says quizzes are 24 hours long, but they're until midnight the same day.
Hardest class I have taken but I learn a lot.

One of the nicest profs Ive ever met. Always available to help, and wants to see you succeed. Grades a little too tough based on the scope of the course (an elective), but makes classes fly by with engaging activities. Overall great prof.

Taking Prof. O'Brien's class was a real treat. She always made the 3.5-4hr lectures fly by with constant engagement and an open-forum-style lecture. Coming from a heavy science background, I can say that her class is the epitome of what I wish all of my electives were. Ask her for support when you need it and she will ALWAYS be willing to help.
